title: History 101

jokes.text: Oh, there's 12 additional thumb tacks on that board. They're driving me crazy.
jokes.setup: Character notices something changed over summer
jokes.punchline: It's an obsessively specific count of thumbtacks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie character moment - her OCD tendencies manifest in hilariously specific ways

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, something's different about my bulletin board.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I had exactly 847 thumbtacks on here before summer break, and now there are 846.

jokes.text: It's cool, but also not.
jokes.setup: Commenting on someone's hipster look
jokes.punchline: Perfectly captures hipster aesthetic contradiction
jokes.explanation: Economical and character-appropriate dig at hipster culture

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what's great about hipsters? They're so committed to being different that they all look exactly the same. It's like they got together and said, 'Let's all shop at the same thrift stores, wear the same vintage glasses, and drink the same overpriced coffee.' You've perfectly captured that contradiction — you're individualistic conformity in human form.

jokes.text: A little late to the hipster party much? A little much on the much much?
jokes.setup: Britta criticizes Jeff's timing on hipster trend
jokes.punchline: Jeff escalates with absurd wordplay critique
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of wordplay that feels naturally competitive between these characters

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You're always three years behind on everything, Jeff. You just discovered skinny jeans.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh, that's rich coming from you. You're so behind on trends, you think 'on fleek' is a medical condition.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That doesn't even make sense.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Exactly. Neither does your fashion sense, and yet here we are.

jokes.text: Damn Mexican cleaning woman did a cha-cha on my glasses
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining why he's wearing old glasses
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific and culturally insensitive explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - casually offensive with absurd imagery, but relies on stereotype

jokes.text: I started out with a babbling brook, but then I layered in elements from our world. I'm sure fans of the babbling brook will complain, but I thought that was limiting.
jokes.setup: Abed describing his meditation visualization
jokes.punchline: He's treating his own meditation like a TV show with fan criticism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary disguised as therapy talk - quintessential Abed viewing everything through TV lens

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm visualizing myself on a TV show, but people keep leaving bad reviews online. 'This character has no depth.' 'The writing is inconsistent.' 'Why does he keep referencing pop culture?'

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm trying to meditate but I keep refreshing the comments section.

jokes.text: Here's the deal, Jessica Biel.
jokes.setup: Jeff addressing Annie
jokes.punchline: Rhyming her name with celebrity for no reason
jokes.explanation: Basic wordplay - functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, you're looking lovely today. You know who you remind me of? Annie Lennox. Yeah, that's right, I went there.

jokes.text: ♪ This is my show, it's about me and all my friends on Abed TV ♪
jokes.setup: Abed going to his 'happy place'
jokes.punchline: His happy place is literally a TV show starring himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment that sets up the episode's meta-structure while being genuinely funny

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm going to my happy place.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's a TV show. Starring me.

jokes.text: Abed's Happy Community College show is filmed before a live audience inside of my head.
jokes.setup: Standard sitcom filming disclaimer
jokes.punchline: It's literally inside his imagination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of sitcom conventions - takes familiar format and makes it absurdly literal

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's literally inside his imagination

jokes.text: I've decided I'm going to do senioritis this year. You know, just hangin' out and blowin' off classes, pullin' pranks, not sayin' my 'G's.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining his senior year plans
jokes.punchline: The last item is the most absurd and specific
jokes.explanation: Great Troy moment - childish misunderstanding of rebellion with specific linguistic quirk

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm going to graduate, get into a good college, maybe study engineering or business, travel Europe for a summer, and learn to make the perfect omelette.

jokes.text: Last year, Troy wished we got Bin Laden and the Dorito Taco. Yeah, but Obama got credit for both.
jokes.setup: Troy claiming their wishes come true
jokes.punchline: Equating major terrorist capture with fast food innovation, plus credit complaint
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - treating trivial and historic events as equally important

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Our wishes come true!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: They captured Osama bin Laden.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: And Subway has a new sandwich!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Also, your credit score went down.

jokes.text: Oh, just like real ice cream.
jokes.setup: Learning the class is first come, first serve
jokes.punchline: Completely obvious observation delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Pierce moment - stating the obvious as if profound

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: So you're saying whoever gets here first gets the class?

jokes.text: I mean, it's progressed, but it hasn't progressed-progressed. It's progressive.
jokes.setup: Being asked about relationship status with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Awkward over-explanation that explains nothing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Annie's nervous over-explanation makes everything more awkward

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Well, it's not like we're... I mean, we're not not... it's just that Jeff and I have this thing where we don't have a thing, but not because we don't want to have a thing, but because having a thing would complicate the thing we already have, which is a non-thing that's better than a thing would be, except when it's not.

jokes.text: I've been pissing in jars for an hour trying to keep this seat.
jokes.setup: Leonard defending his seat in class
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely extreme method of seat-saving
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disgusting Leonard moment - commitment to grossness is character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Leonard
dialogue.line: This is my seat. I sit here every class. You can't just take someone's seat.

dialogue.speaker: Leonard
dialogue.line: I've marked it. With my blood.

jokes.text: This can't be good. He's dressed as himself.
jokes.setup: Dean appears in normal clothes
jokes.punchline: Implication that Dean's normal appearance is more concerning than his costumes
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that inverts expectations - Dean being normal is the abnormal thing

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, Dean, I have to say, seeing you in normal clothes is somehow more disturbing than any of your costumes.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Because now I know this is a choice.

jokes.text: Someone hacked into the supply closet and counterfeited these 'History of Ice Cream' admission cards.
jokes.setup: Dean announces registration system has been hacked
jokes.punchline: The 'hacking' was just physical counterfeiting in a supply closet
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up high-tech crime but reveals low-tech solution

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Our registration system has been hacked.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: We're investigating the breach as we speak.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: It turns out the 'hacking' was just someone counterfeiting registration forms in a supply closet.

jokes.text: The Hunger Deans!
jokes.setup: Dean announcing competition format
jokes.punchline: Pun on Hunger Games with Dean's name, plus assumed costume/staging
jokes.explanation: Typical Dean pun plus visual spectacle - character-appropriate but not groundbreaking

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Welcome to the Hunger Games... I mean, the Hunger Deans!

jokes.text: And these are impossible to counterfeit because each one has been marked by my distinct bite print.
jokes.setup: Dean explaining security measures for red balls
jokes.punchline: His personal teeth marks as anti-counterfeiting measure
jokes.explanation: Gloriously weird Dean logic - solving problems in the most inappropriate way possible

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: We've implemented several security measures for the red balls. Holograms, serial numbers, and as a final anti-counterfeiting measure... my personal teeth marks.

jokes.text: Well, graduating after 30 can't be characterized as early.
jokes.setup: Annie asking if Jeff is graduating early
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dry acknowledgment of his age situation
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about Jeff's age among college students

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I'm 14 years old, so graduating early would be a medical miracle.

jokes.text: F-i-n-e or f-y-n-e? We made one of 'em a code for 'not fine.'
jokes.setup: Abed says he's 'fine'
jokes.punchline: Troy has created an unnecessarily complex spelling-based code system
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully overcomplicated Troy solution to simple problem - both sweet and absurd

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Okay, so if you're fine, that means you take the first letter of the word, multiply it by the number of letters in the word, and then—

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Troy, no.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: —subtract the vowels, and that's your code!

jokes.text: Like an ice cream cone melting in the sun, our time here can't last forever.
jokes.setup: Jeff making grand speech about leaving
jokes.punchline: Overly sentimental metaphor that ties to ice cream class
jokes.explanation: Jeff's tendency toward overwrought speeches - the metaphor is both appropriate and cringily earnest

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know, leaving Greendale is like... it's like finishing the last scoop of ice cream. You think it's going to last forever, but then one day you look down and the cone is empty. And you realize that every lick, every single moment of that ice cream, was precious.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So I'm not saying goodbye. I'm just... finishing my cone.

jokes.text: That was part of the speech I was writing!
jokes.setup: Jeff delivers emotional metaphor
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was pre-planned speechifying, not genuine emotion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Jeff's manipulative nature - even his emotions are calculated

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know, life is like a journey. We're all just passengers on a ship, sailing through an ocean of uncertainty, hoping to find our way to shore.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And sometimes, we need to lean on each other, to be there for one another, because that's what truly matters in this world.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Actually, I prepared this speech three weeks ago. I've been waiting for the right moment to use it.

jokes.text: Close. I am America's sexiest aviator, Leo DiCaprio.
jokes.setup: Someone guesses his costume is Amelia Earhart
jokes.punchline: Dean's convoluted costume logic invoking Leonardo DiCaprio
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean costume logic - overly specific reference that makes no visual sense

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I'm Leonardo DiCaprio. See, he was in Inception, which was about dreams. Amelia Earhart disappeared, which is like a dream because it's something that never really happened. So I'm Leonardo DiCaprio dressed as a dream of Amelia Earhart.

jokes.text: Yeah, Amelia Earhart would have been faster.
jokes.setup: Dean's elaborate costume explanation
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Earhart's mysterious disappearance
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered perfectly deadpan - unexpected from Shirley makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I'm dressed as Amelia Earhart. She disappeared over the Pacific Ocean in 1937, and to this day, no one knows what happened to her.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: That's so sad.

jokes.text: As you know, our student records are stored on a Microsoft Paint file, which I was assured was future-proof.
jokes.setup: Dean explaining lost student records
jokes.punchline: School's critical data stored in basic drawing program
jokes.explanation: Perfect Greendale incompetence - absurd but believable for this school's tech capabilities

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Our most critical data is stored in Microsoft Paint.

jokes.text: Meanwhile, our extended warranty is not being honored by the restaurant that used to be Circuit City.
jokes.setup: Explaining technical difficulties
jokes.punchline: Chain of business failures affecting their warranty claim
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of institutional dysfunction - perfectly captures economic decline

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Well, you see, there's a chain of business failures. First, the manufacturer went out of business, then the distributor, then the retailer where you bought it. So technically, nobody's around to honor the warranty.

jokes.text: In-Dean, I am.
jokes.setup: Being asked if he's serious about repeating three years
jokes.punchline: Dean pun on 'indeed'
jokes.explanation: Standard Dean pun - character-appropriate but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Indeed. In-deed. Like, a deed. Inside me. I'm going to be here for three years.

jokes.text: I'm gonna be here forever. Forever. Forever. Forever.
jokes.setup: Realizing they have to repeat three years
jokes.punchline: Increasingly manic repetition showing mental breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good character breakdown moment - Annie's perfectionism creates genuine panic

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: We have to repeat three years. Three years. We have to repeat three years. Three years we have to repeat. Repeat three years we have to. Years three repeat we have to. Have to repeat years three we. Three years repeat to have we...

jokes.text: You? You're pre-med! I want ice cream.
jokes.setup: Jeff facing unexpected competition in physical challenge
jokes.punchline: Pre-med student's motivation is just wanting ice cream
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - the simple motivation makes the competition more absurd

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wait, you're doing this just for ice cream?

dialogue.speaker: Competitor
dialogue.line: Yeah, they said winner gets a gift card to Marble Slab.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm losing to a pre-med student whose only motivation is frozen dairy.

jokes.text: Hey, did you know you could major in Antics? I checked. You need a tomfoolery pre-req, but they waive it for women.
jokes.setup: Discussing changing majors if they restart
jokes.punchline: Fake major with sexist waiver policy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake academia joke with subtle social commentary - perfectly ridiculous course catalog

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: If we restart, I'm changing majors. I'm thinking about going into... I don't know, something that doesn't require me to think about my future.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I don't know. Hospitality? Event planning?

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: We actually have a new major for that. It's called 'Wife Studies.'

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That's... that's not a real major.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: It is now. And it comes with a sexist waiver policy.

jokes.text: He's gonna come in here later and have the creepy feeling someone was in here.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining his 'prank' of just being in Dean's office
jokes.punchline: The prank is creating vague unease, not actual mischief
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic - misunderstanding what makes pranks effective in the most innocent way

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm in the Dean's office right now. Just sitting here. He doesn't know why I'm here. He keeps asking me questions, but I don't answer them. I just stare. It's the perfect prank because there's nothing he can actually complain about.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Troy, why are you in my office?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: No answer. Just the stare. The beauty of it is the ambiguity. He's going to spend all day wondering what I'm up to, and I'm not up to anything. That's the prank.

jokes.text: Let's move everything on his desk over an inch except his stapler. Then he'll think we moved his stapler.
jokes.setup: Britta wants to actually do something for the prank
jokes.punchline: Troy's 'prank on a prank' is unnecessarily complex psychological manipulation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of prank logic - Troy's mind works in wonderfully convoluted ways

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: We should actually do something for the prank.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh, I have a prank on a prank. I'm going to convince Jeff that Abed convinced me to prank him, but really I'm pranking Abed by making him think his prank worked when actually I'm going to swap out all his DVDs with bootleg copies, so when he tries to watch them, they skip at a crucial emotional moment, making him think they're damaged, so he'll buy new ones, but I've already bought the new ones and hidden them in his room so he'll find them and think he's crazy.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, there's so many levels to this.
jokes.setup: Troy's multi-layered prank explanation
jokes.punchline: Britta's amazement at the unnecessary complexity
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that amplifies the absurdity of Troy's overcomplicated prank

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Wait, you did all that just to make me think Troy was dead?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: It was a prank!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That's insane! You could've just jumped out and yelled 'Boo!'

jokes.text: That sounds messy! (excited)
jokes.setup: Britta suggests filling car with popcorn, Troy initially objects
jokes.punchline: His tone completely changes - mess is actually appealing to him
jokes.explanation: Good character flip - Troy's childlike enthusiasm overrides practical concerns

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. A car full of popcorn? That's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Come on, it'll be fun!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: No, it's... it's a mess, it's a fire hazard, it's—

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Actually, hold on. A car full of popcorn? That's amazing. Let's do it right now.

jokes.text: Heels. Oh, you got skills.
jokes.setup: Troy finding women's heels in Dean's office
jokes.punchline: His impressed reaction to presumed cross-dressing skills
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction - Troy's non-judgmental appreciation of Dean's fashion choices

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Dean Pelton, you sly dog. I had no idea you were into this kind of thing. These are some serious heels. And they fit you perfectly? That's impressive. Most men couldn't pull off this look, but you're really working it. I respect the confidence.

jokes.text: Oh, no, it's cool. It's Leonard's.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about blood on Jeff's shirt
jokes.punchline: Casual confirmation that he bloodied another student in competition
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation played casually - Jeff's competitiveness has turned violent

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh, this? Yeah, I may have gotten a little competitive in dodgeball.

jokes.text: The first wish is always for 1,000 wishes.
jokes.setup: Britta makes a sincere first wish
jokes.punchline: Troy reveals she's violated fundamental wishing protocol
jokes.explanation: Good callback to childhood wish logic - Troy's serious about silly rules

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I wish for world peace.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! You can't just wish for world peace! That's like wishing for more wishes. It's the first rule of wishing!

jokes.text: No wishes containing the word 'all.' Guaranteed ironic consequences.
jokes.setup: Britta wishes to end all wars
jokes.punchline: Troy has thought through wish-making safety protocols
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic applied to fantasy - Troy understands genre conventions better than expected

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I wish all wars would end.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. You can't just wish for all wars to end without thinking through the consequences. What about all the soldiers who are gonna suddenly be out of work? What about the weapons manufacturers? You're gonna crash the global economy!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: It's just a wish, Troy.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: A wish with no safety protocols is how you end up with a monkey's paw situation, Britta.

jokes.text: Star Wars, Thumb Wars, wow, Storage Wars!
jokes.setup: Explaining what 'all wars' would eliminate
jokes.punchline: Escalation from epic to parody to reality TV
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with perfect Troy enthusiasm - treats all 'wars' as equally important

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: If all wars were fought like this, it would eliminate... escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You'd go from epic to parody to reality TV in like twenty minutes.

jokes.text: You can't flip a wish. That's ridiculous. Your rules are ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Britta and Troy arguing about wish mechanics
jokes.punchline: Britta's frustration with arbitrary but elaborate rule system
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict - Britta's rationality vs. Troy's elaborate fantasy rules

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You can't just wish for more wishes, Troy! That's not how it works!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Why not? Who decided that?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Everyone! It's a rule. There are rules to wishing.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Says who?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Says... the universe! You can't just go around wishing for more wishes like some kind of wish anarchist!

jokes.text: Unwish! Ununwish. Rewish.
jokes.setup: Britta and Troy's fountain fight
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd made-up wish-reversal terminology
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with creative wordplay - childish fight taken to logical extreme

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I wish I'd never said that!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Too late. You've been Britta'd.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That's not even a real thing!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Now you're getting Britta'd squared.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Stop it!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh, you want to un-Britta yourself? That'll cost you a Britta-reversal fee, plus interest. We're talking Britta'd to the power of Britta.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: This is ridiculous!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Ridiculous? That's a Britta-and-a-half right there. You're looking at a full Britta-ectomy at this point.

jokes.text: Ah, great! My 'G's are back.
jokes.setup: Troy gets emotional during fight with Britta
jokes.punchline: His senioritis affectation disappears when he's genuinely upset
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Troy's fake rebellion can't survive real emotion

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You know what, Britta? I'm done pretending I don't care about anything!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You actually hurt my feelings, and that's... that's not cool.

jokes.text: Can't wait to be buried alive under a pile of paperwork and bed pans, just trying to summon the courage to ask out Dr. Patel, the gorgeous Indian neurosurgeon who doesn't even know I exist.
jokes.setup: Shirley sarcastically expressing enthusiasm for hospital administration
jokes.punchline: Detailed vision of soul-crushing job with specific romantic subplot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Shirley's specific fears about her future are both funny and sad

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Oh yes, I'm so excited about hospital administration. I can already picture myself: middle-aged, divorced, having an affair with a radiologist named Dennis who smells like old coffee and broken dreams. We'll meet in the supply closet every Thursday at 2 PM, and he'll tell me I'm 'different' from his wife. By 2025, I'll be wearing exclusively beige and won't remember what joy feels like.

jokes.text: This isn't a conversation, is it?
jokes.setup: Shirley's elaborate future vision monologue
jokes.punchline: Troy realizes he's accidentally triggered a soliloquy
jokes.explanation: Good meta-moment - character recognizing they've stumbled into monologue territory

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I can see it now. In five years, I'll have expanded my sandwich business into a full restaurant empire. I'll be on the cover of Black Enterprise magazine. My children will be at Ivy League schools. I'll have a summer home in Martha's Vineyard and winter home in Boca Raton.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That sounds great, Shirley. Hey, what do you think about—

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: And I'll finally get that kitchen with the double ovens, the marble countertops, the Sub-Zero refrigerator that I've always dreamed of. My husband will have learned to appreciate my culinary genius. Our love will be stronger than ever, forged in the fires of entrepreneurial success and—

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh no. Oh no no no. I opened the Shirley vision box. I can't close it. Why did I ask?

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! The sad, slow march toward death begins. Senioritis!
jokes.setup: Troy trying to maintain senioritis enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully celebrating existential dread
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic - maintains enthusiasm even when describing mortality

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We're seniors! We're basically already gone. Nothing matters anymore!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Woop woop! Existential dread!

jokes.text: A big man. A huge, throbbing, sweaty... Dean, I just want to get my history credit and get out of here.
jokes.setup: Dean building to sexual innuendo
jokes.punchline: Jeff cuts him off before the inappropriate climax
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup with character-appropriate interruption - Jeff knows where this is going

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: I need you to come into my office and help me with something long and hard.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, what is it?

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: It's a wooden beam I'm trying to—

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm gonna stop you right there.

jokes.text: I choose... The Dean.
jokes.setup: Jeff choosing tango partner for competition
jokes.punchline: Chooses the judge instead of logical partner
jokes.explanation: Good strategic misdirection - Jeff manipulating the system by seducing the judge

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm going with her. She's clearly the best dancer here.

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: I'm judging this competition.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: The fountain works.
jokes.setup: Jeff successfully manipulates Dean through tango partnership
jokes.punchline: Troy credits their wish-making fountain for the success
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Troy's magical thinking gets seeming validation

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You know what? I think we should throw a coin in the wish-making fountain and thank it for helping us win.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Troy, that doesn't make any sense. I manipulated the Dean through strategy and charm.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Jeff, the fountain has never let us down. Every wish we've thrown in there has come true.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not how fountains work.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Tell that to my wish for a mint condition copy of Lee Majors' autobiography. I got it three days later.

jokes.text: That's your penis.
jokes.setup: Jeff showing how close his fingers are together
jokes.punchline: Pierce's crude observation about the gesture
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce juvenile humor - character-appropriate but low-hanging fruit

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That's what my wife said.

jokes.text: ♪ Greendale babies, we're happy and we're free ♪
jokes.setup: Abed retreating further into fantasy sitcom
jokes.punchline: Show becomes even more infantile - now they're literal babies
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Abed's coping mechanism - regression to ultimate safety

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I can't take this anymore. I'm retreating into a fantasy sitcom world.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? Make it even more infantile. We're all literal babies now.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Goo goo ga ga.

jokes.text: Gay balls! Nailed it.
jokes.setup: Pierce commenting on something ball-related
jokes.punchline: Juvenile double entendre with self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Peak Pierce juvenile humor - character-appropriate but lazy wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That's what she said. I still got it.

jokes.text: Do you realize who you told that to?
jokes.setup: Learning the therapist told Abed to go to his happy place
jokes.punchline: Recognition that this advice is particularly dangerous for Abed
jokes.explanation: Good character awareness - Britta understands Abed's unique psychology better than the therapist

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Wait, the therapist told you to go to your happy place?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Yeah, why?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Abed, your happy place is a TV show.

jokes.text: God, I hate new Jeff.
jokes.setup: Jeff continuing to compete despite claiming to help friends
jokes.punchline: Britta's exasperation with Jeff's supposed character growth
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing New Jeff is just as selfish as Old Jeff, maybe worse

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not competing. I'm just here to support you guys.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Jeff, you've said that exact thing five times while actively sabotaging everyone.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's called multitasking, Britta.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No, that's called being a terrible person who learned the word 'growth' and thinks it makes you one.

jokes.text: Even if we go somewhere, we're not going anywhere.
jokes.setup: Jeff's speech about friendship enduring despite change
jokes.punchline: Paradoxical statement that sounds profound but is confusing
jokes.explanation: Typical Jeff pseudo-profound wordplay - sounds meaningful but doesn't quite make sense

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what I've learned? Friendship is like a river. It's always changing, always flowing, but it's still the same river. Or it's a completely different river and we're all just going crazy.

jokes.text: I literally just walked up. I know. I made the speech for you.
jokes.setup: Jeff delivers emotional speech to save Abed
jokes.punchline: Abed reveals Jeff didn't actually say anything - it was performed for the audience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-revelation - Abed orchestrated the entire emotional climax from his fantasy

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Abed, I need you to listen to me. You're my friend, and I care about you more than I care about myself. You've always been there for me, and I'm going to be there for you now.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Jeff, that was a really beautiful speech.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Thanks. I meant every word.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: No, I mean, that was beautiful for the audience. You didn't actually say anything to me. You just gave a speech directly to the camera.

jokes.text: When they were incepting, I got their balls.
jokes.setup: Chang appearing with red balls while others were in Abed's fantasy
jokes.punchline: Reference to Inception plus juvenile ball humor
jokes.explanation: Decent Chang moment combining pop culture reference with his opportunistic nature

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: We need to go deeper.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: You smell like the floor of a movie theater. Yeah, but not for the usual reasons.
jokes.setup: Jeff commenting on Dean's smell
jokes.punchline: Dean's clarification makes it worse by implying usual reasons are sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean inappropriate overshare - makes innocent comment sexual

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Dean, you smell like a cocktail.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: That's my cologne. It's called Abstinence.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Well, it's not working.

jokes.text: Now we'll be like almost roomies.
jokes.setup: Dean revealing he bought condo near Jeff
jokes.punchline: Diminutive language trying to make stalking sound cute
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean stalker behavior disguised as friendly enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I bought a little condo just a teensy bit down the street from you. I'm just a hop, skip, and a jump away! We're practically neighbors!

jokes.text: Get ready to run. In these heels?
jokes.setup: Troy and Britta caught in cross-dressing prank
jokes.punchline: Britta's practical concern about running in women's shoes
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy - practical concern in absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: How am I supposed to run in these heels?

jokes.text: They're fabulous. They're charming.
jokes.setup: Security guard catching cross-dressed students
jokes.punchline: His appreciation for their fashion choices instead of arrest
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - expected authority response subverted by fashion appreciation

dialogue.speaker: Security Guard
dialogue.line: Hold it right there! You two are in clear violation of dress code policy.

dialogue.speaker: Student 1
dialogue.line: We can explain—

dialogue.speaker: Security Guard
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I'm not here to lecture you. I just want to say... that dress is absolutely fabulous on you. And those heels? *Chef's kiss.* Where did you get them?

jokes.text: Okay, I know a man when I see one.
jokes.setup: Security guard commenting on cross-dressed students
jokes.punchline: Implication that he's attracted and has experience in this area
jokes.explanation: Standard gender-confusion humor - functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Security Guard
dialogue.line: You know, I've seen a lot of things in my time as a security guard. But these outfits... they really know what they're doing.

dialogue.speaker: Security Guard
dialogue.line: Not that I would know anything about that. But if I did... let's just say I'd have some notes.

tmdb_overview: As Jeff competes for spots in an overbooked 'History of Ice Cream' class, Abed copes with the looming end of their time at Greendale by retreating into a surreal mental 'happy place', while Annie and Shirley engage in school-wide pranks.
insight: Season 4 stumbles out the gate with 60 jokes but minimal landing power.
